data.title: Autris signs term sheet to acquire bitcoin treasury company
data.description: • Autris (OTCPK:AUTR) announced on Friday the  signing of a term sheet to acquire a Bitcoin Treasury company to further enhance its Bitcoin treasury strategy.

data.title: Allegiant sees July passenger traffic increase 10.3%
data.description:
• Allegiant Travel (NASDAQ:ALGT) on Friday reported a 10.3% increase in scheduled service in July nearly to 2.09M, driving up the total number of miles flown by paying passengers (revenue passenger miles) by 10.3% to 1.86M.
  • Available seat miles rose around 11.5% to 2.14M.
  • ALGT +0.17% premarket to $62.41.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 5 stocks to watch on Friday: BABA, PEP, ULTA, APLD, CAT
data.description:
Stock index futures pared some losses on Friday, as the July PCE report came in-line. Here are five stocks to watch:

American Depositary Shares of Alibaba (BABA) shares were up 4.4% in premarket trade as the firm has reportedly developed a new chip that is more versatile than its older chips. The new chip, which is now in testing, is meant to serve a broader range of AI inference tasks, the report added.

PepsiCo (PEP) was up 0.2% in extended trading as it struck a deal to increase its ownership stake in Celsius Holdings through a $585 million deal. Sources indicate that the move will see PepsiCo's (PEP) stake grow to 11% after the purchase of convertible preferred stock, and the food and beverage giant will have the right to nominate another director to the Celsius board of directors.

Ulta Beauty (ULTA) shares were up 3.6% in premarket trade after the beauty retailer raised its outlook for the full year, reflecting another better-than-expected quarter and progress in its “Ulta Beauty Unleashed” strategy.

Applied Digital (APLD) was up 2.2% before the market open, as it has signed a new long-term lease with CoreWeave (CRWV) for 150MW of additional data center capacity at its Polaris Forge 1 Campus in Ellendale, North Dakota. This deal increases Applied Digital’s total expected contracted lease revenue to about $11 billion, with CoreWeave now set to occupy 400MW across three buildings at the site.

Caterpillar (CAT) fell 2.4% in premarket trade after disclosing it now expects the net impact from tariffs in the $1.5B-$1.8B range this year, up from a previous estimate of $1.3B-$1.5B, as well as a $500M-$600M impact in Q3, compared to its earlier outlook for $400M-$500M.

data.title: UBS starts bullish coverage on Karooooo
data.description:
UBS initiated its coverage of Karooooo (NASDAQ:KARO) with a Buy rating and a price target of $60, implying an upside of more than 14%.

The brokerage firm is modeling an acceleration to 19% Y/Y revenue growth in FY26 from 15% Y/Y in FY25, arguing that the company has a significant opportunity to capitalize on early-stage cross-sell opportunities.

UBS analyst Taylor McGinnis noted that Karooooo is well positioned to sustain high-teens growth as it strategically targets underserved markets, scales sales, capitalizes on early cross-sell opportunities and benefits from regulatory, safety and security initiatives that offer additional tailwinds.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings, Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street analysts are bullish about the firm and rated as Buy.

Shares of KARO, a telematics, fleet management and delivery-as-a-service company, have gained over 16% so far this year, outperforming the broader S&P 500 Index’s nearly 10.5% gain during the same period.

data.title: Compagnie Financière Tradition SA reports 1H results
data.description:
• Compagnie Financière Tradition SA press release (OTCPK:CFNCF): 1H Growth in Revenue to CHF 632.1 million, up 12.3% at constant exchange rates (+9.6% at current exchange rates)
  • Operating profit before depreciation and amortization (EBITDA) increase to CHF 114.7 million, up 27.3% at constant exchange rates. 
  • Net profit Group share of CHF 70.2 million, up 20.4% at constant exchange rates (+17.0% at current exchange rates)

data.title: ServiceTitan in spotlight as Raymond James starts with Market Perform rating
data.description:
• ServiceTitan (NASDAQ:TTAN) was in the spotlight on Friday as Raymond James started the software company with a Market Perform rating.
  • “We believe the company has all the makings of an elite vertical software company, given its strong position serving a fragmented customer base; high ROI value proposition for customers that supports a 110%+ net revenue retention (NRR) profile; and opportunity to expand efficiently into other trades,” analyst Brian Peterson wrote in a note to clients. “This would suggest that the company’s durable growth rate is higher than the rest of our software coverage, and worthy of a valuation premium. However, with shares up 52% since the IPO and trading at 10x sales (vs. the vertical software group at 7x) we await a more attractive entry point.”
  • Shares were modestly higher in premarket trading.

data.title: Ambarella surges after 'strong' Q2, rating upgrade at Summit
data.description:
Shares of Ambarella (NASDAQ:AMBA) jumped about 17% premarket on Friday after second quarter results and guidance exceeded estimates, while also seeing a rating upgrade at Summit Insights.

Summit upgraded the semiconductor company's stock to Buy from Hold. The firm said that autonomous robotics and AI-enabled portable cameras mean total addressable market, or TAM, expansion for Ambarella.

Analyst Kinngai Chan said that Ambarella's growth continues to be driven by the ramp of its higher ASP CV-based system-on-a-chips, or SoCs. The analyst believes the near-term upside was also driven by tariff-related customer pull-ins.

"While we still expect its automotive business to lag its edge IoT business, the emergence of autonomous robotics coupled with the renewed popularity for portable 360° cameras can meaningfully expand the TAM for its IoT business," said Chan. "We see this expanded TAM as an inflection point and expect AMBA to deliver further outperformance into FY27."

Oppenheimer maintained its Perform rating on Ambarella.

Analyst Martin Yang said that Ambarella's management highlighted strength in the portable video market and upcoming high-volume win in autonomous aerial drones. Insta360, the world's largest 360-camera maker, is driving demand growth and product innovation in those markets that's benefited Ambarella. 

For edge AI infra-market, the company announced its first design win with a networking equipment maker. Revenue from this N1-655 win is expected sometime in 2026, the analyst added.

"We're encouraged by AMBA's recent towline growth momentum, but stay sidelined for more evidence of earnings growth acceleration," said Yang.

Morgan Stanley kept its Overweight rating and raised the price target on the stock to $96 from $80.

"Delivered another strong quarter from Edge AI strength. While waiting for autonomous automotive enthusiasm to resume, edge-based AI in other applications is emerging as the clear near-term growth driver," said analysts led by Joseph Moore.

The analysts noted that Ambarella is seeing strong demand for edge AI. Portable video, robotics (including aerial drones ramping in late FY26), and emerging edge infrastructure are now contributing to their bullish outlook.

data.title: Footwear funeral: Skechers prepares to go private after 26 years on the NYSE
data.description:
The buyout of Skechers U.S.A. (NYSE:SKX) by 3G Capital has landed all the necessary regulatory approvals and is expected to close on September 12. 

3G Capital struck the $9.4 billion deal with Skechers (NYSE:SKX) at $63 per share in cash or a combination of $57 per share plus one equity unit in a new, private parent company.

3G Capital's acquisition of Skechers (SKX) marks one of the largest privatizations in the footwear industry, removing the company from the public market after 26 years. Looking ahead, 3G Capital aims to leverage its operational efficiency expertise to boost Skechers' (SKX) global growth, innovation, and infrastructure investment.

The $9.4 billion price tag for Skechers (SKX) is slightly below the $9.7 billion market cap for Birkenstock (BIRK) and well above the market caps on Crocs (CROX), Wolverine World Wide (WWW), Steven Madden (SHOO), and Weyco Group (WEYS).

Skechers (SKX) went public on June 9, 1999, on the New York Stock Exchange at an opening price of $11 per share. The all-time high closing price was $78.24 on January 30, 2025. Skechers (SKX) is currently swapping hands for just a few pennies over the $63 deal price.

data.title: NeoGenomics rallies after court win in Natera patent dispute
data.description:
• NeoGenomics (NASDAQ:NEO) shares climbed ~20% in the premarket on Friday after the company announced a major victory in a legal battle over a patent dispute with its rival cancer test developer Natera (NASDAQ:NTRA).
  • In a statement on Friday, the company stated that a federal judge in North Carolina granted its motion for summary judgment after determining that Natera’s (NASDAQ:NTRA) asserted patents were invalid due to claims of trivial subject matter.
  • The District Court for the Middle District of North Carolina has stated it will dismiss the NTRA’s claims with prejudice, meaning that another case over the same claims cannot be brought back to the court.
  • The judgment allows NeoGenomics (NASDAQ:NEO) to commercialize its cancer test RaDaR ST assay, formerly known as RaDaR 1.1, without any constraints in the U.S.

data.title: Hawkins buys StillWaters Technology
data.description:
• Hawkins (NASDAQ:HWKN) has acquired StillWaters Technology to further expand its water treatment business and build out its southern U.S. presence.
  • StillWaters distributes water treatment chemicals and equipment for its customers in Alabama.
  • Source: Press Release
